raw_response:
I agree with the consensus that Clonidine (Option B) is the safest antihypertensive during pregnancy among the provided options. One additional point supporting this view is that Clonidine crosses the placenta minimally, reducing the likelihood of exposing the developing fetus to any substance that might cause harm directly upon crossing into fetal circulation. This property adds another layer of safety beyond what was previously discussed, reinforcing its appropriateness as the first-line medication for acute hypertensive emergencies in pregnant women.

Final answer: **B**
